sglang.title: add cuda graph capture failure possible solution
sglang.author: zhyncs
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
When using EAGLE 2 speculative decoding, it may fail to start up due to a CUDA graph capture failure. Consider reducing the CUDA graph max batch size (default is 160).

Modifications

Checklist

 Format your code according to the Code Formatting with Pre-Commit.
 Add unit tests as outline

sglang.title: fix draft cuda graph capture failure
sglang.author: zhyncs
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
fix Capture cuda graph failed: mat1 and mat2 must have the same dtype, but got Float and Half
python3 -m sglang.launch_server --model meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B-Instruct  \
--speculative-algo EAGLE --speculative-draft lmzheng/sglang-EAGLE-LLaMA3-Instruct-8B  \
--speculative-num-steps 5 --

sglang.title: [Fix] Fix accuracy bug and refactor codes for lora
sglang.author: Fridge003
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

#2671 reports a bug about output misalignment when applying Lora. The bug is actually caused by incomplete lora support on qkv_proj and gate_up_proj modules, which should be fixed by this PR.
Also, this PR refactors the structure of lora codes to increase code readability.
Modifications

vllm.title: [MISC] Always import version library first in the vllm package
vllm.author: houseroad
vllm.bodyExcerpt: We insert some customization logic into the generated _version.py. We need to ensure that the "version" library is always the first one to import in the vllm package.

vllm.title: [core] improve error handling when wake up from sleep mode
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Feedback from RLHF user: when vLLM wakes up from sleep mode, and there's not enough memory, there will be an error from C++ side, with error messages:
CUDA Error: out of memory at /workspace/csrc/cumem_allocator.cpp:56
CUDA Error: invalid argument at /workspace/csrc/cumem_allocator.cpp:57
CUDA Error

vllm.title: [core][rlhf] add colocate example for RLHF
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt: requested from the RLHF community, add an example to show how to use cuda-ipc to sync weights from training actors to vllm workers, when they are co-located on the same set of GPUs.

vllm.title: [V1] Use msgpack for core request serialization
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
We were using pickle rather than msgpack for serializing the messages from the front-end process to the engine, because msgpack doesn't natively support tensors and these need to be included for multimodal requests.
These can be handled easily though via custom msgpack extension. This should mean mo

vllm.title: [Bugfix][Platform] Check whether selected backend is None in get_attn_backend_cls()
vllm.author: terrytangyuan
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This avoids the misleading message "cannot use None backend" as seen below:
INFO 02-09 03:13:01 __init__.py:190] Automatically detected platform cpu.
INFO 02-09 03:13:05 cpu.py:39] Cannot use None backend on CPU.
INFO 02-09 03:13:05 cpu.py:40] Using Torch SDPA backend.

vllm.title: [core] fix sleep mode and pytorch checkpoint compatibility
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
reported from the RLHF community, that using pytorch checkpoint is incompatible with the sleep mode, because of the torch.cuda.empty_cache() call.
see verl-project/verl#209 (comment) for details.

vllm.title: [Doc] Add link to tool_choice tracking issue in tool_calling.md
vllm.author: terrytangyuan
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This adds a link to the tracking issue so that others can upvote and comment for feedback.
